codeReview.summary: All 6 items in the feature description are correctly implemented: (C1) Dashboard edit page CATEGORY_OPTIONS updated to Title Case, shared categories.ts updated; (I3) .max(255) added to brandName in register.ts; (I11) null guard added after findUnique post-mint returning 500; (C5) PUT added to MUTATING_METHODS in csrf.ts; (C4) SELECT FOR UPDATE claims removed from mint.ts comments and README.md, replaced with optimistic concurrency control; (ENV) SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD added to .env.example. Two new tests added (brandName max length, PUT CSRF middleware). All 186 tests pass, typecheck and build clean.

issues.description: Shared CATEGORIES includes 'Fragrances' and 'Eyewear' which are not in the API's PRODUCT_CATEGORIES (create.ts/update.ts only accept: Leather Goods, Jewelry, Watches, Fashion, Accessories, Other). Code using the shared Category type could construct values the API rejects. Consider aligning or documenting the difference.

PRODUCT_CATEGORIES is defined locally in both create.ts and update.ts rather than imported from @galileo/shared. This is pre-existing duplication that was not addressed. If shared/categories.ts is the source of truth, these should import from it (or vice versa).

PUT CSRF test uses mock objects rather than app.inject() integration test (unlike the PATCH CSRF test above it). This is pragmatically necessary since no PUT routes exist yet, but it's less robust — it tests the middleware function in isolation rather than the actual Fastify hook pipeline.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The API route files (create.ts, update.ts) define their own PRODUCT_CATEGORIES arrays locally with 6 values, while the shared package exports CATEGORIES with 8 values (includes Fragrances, Eyewear). This mismatch means the shared type Category allows values the API rejects. A library note documenting the canonical category list and where it's defined would prevent future confusion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/api/src/routes/products/create.ts:10 defines 6 categories; packages/shared/src/constants/categories.ts:10 defines 8 categories including Fragrances and Eyewear not in the API enum.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted that the backend-worker skill suggests curl testing against a running API, but for validation-focused changes, unit tests via inject() were sufficient and more reliable. Skill could note that curl testing is optional when unit tests fully cover behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The backend-worker skill says to test with curl against a running API. For this feature (validation fixes), unit tests via inject() were sufficient and more reliable.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that DATABASE_URL_TEST defaults to postgresql://postgres@localhost:5432/galileo_test which fails on macOS where the default user is the OS user. Tests required manually setting DATABASE_URL_TEST. This is a pre-existing issue not in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues: 'The test global-setup.ts defaults to postgresql://postgres@localhost:5432/galileo_test which fails on macOS where the default user is the OS user.'

summary: All 6 fixes in the feature description are correctly implemented with appropriate tests. No blocking issues found. Three non-blocking observations: (1) shared categories module has 2 extra categories not accepted by the API, (2) PRODUCT_CATEGORIES is duplicated across route files instead of imported from shared, (3) PUT CSRF test uses mock approach since no PUT routes exist. The worker followed the skill procedure, ran all verification steps (tests, typecheck, build), and produced a clean commit. PASS.
